text:
Once upon a time, in a forest not too far from a small village, there lived a wise old owl named Olive. She knew a lot about everything. She especially loved listening to the birds. There were so many different kinds. They sang all sorts of things. There were warblers. They sang songs like humans. They weren…Oh dear.

Olive noticed something odd. For days, warblers hadn’t sung. They just fluttered quietly in the leaves. She flew down to investigate one morning and found them all hunched like owls in candlelight. They had yellow feathers and heavy chests, but nobody was opening their mouths.

Olive hopped across the branch, her mind racing. She couldn’t think. What might happen? She couldn’t just sit there! Something must be wrong. She swooped down, perched on a branch.

“Hello?”

No reply came. She hooted softly, hoping.

Nothing. Still nothing.

She couldn’t sleep. She
